steps.title: Read the imagined alternative
steps.content: The subjects looked where the target ball would have gone if the other ball had not altered its path. That means they were reasoning about an alternative outcome under different conditions from what actually happened.

steps.title: Name that reasoning
steps.content: Choice A describes mixed feelings, not causal reasoning. Choice C means reasoning by comparison. Choice D means linking ideas through association. The remaining word names thinking about what would have happened if the facts had been different. The answer is B.
